digest:
Knowmarks is a local-first, open-source knowledge store serving as a shared substrate for humans and AI agents. It ingests everything—URLs, PDFs, Office docs, YouTube, GitHub, Readwise, browser clips, AI conversation threads, email, and local files—into a single format-agnostic corpus. Over this corpus sit three ownership-clean surfaces: Notes (user-authored markdown), Wiki (LLM-compiled under strict-compiler discipline with every claim traced to a source span), and Graph (pipeline-extracted entities and edges with per-claim provenance). Spaces unify projects, clusters, and curated collections into one composable type with origin, membership rule, and lifetime-intent attributes.

The MCP server is the primary agent interface, offering 35 tools for CRUD, governance, and retrieval with a surface cascade (wiki first, graph second, passages third) that honors token budgets. The Companion is the conversational human surface, wrapping MCP verbs in confirm-flow language to handle triage, intake mediation, and retrieval preview. CLI, REST API, web dashboard, browser extension, and macOS desktop app remain peer surfaces. The system uses hybrid search (vector + FTS5 + RRF) with personalized relevance, automatic clustering, and freshness monitoring via source-aware staleness probes.

Several major surfaces are proposed but not yet built. The Wiki compiler (strict-compiler discipline, per-paragraph citations, coverage indicators) and Graph entity resolution layer (canonical IDs, merge/split history, negative evidence) are target behavior. Passage-level retrieval for long-form items (chunking, passage embeddings) is active but the citation verbs are proposed. The Global Launcher (system-wide ⌘⇧K shortcut) and in-document highlighting overlay (recogito, W3C selectors) are proposed. Email and filesystem connectors are proposed. Docling is proposed as an evaluation-gated replacement for the current PDF/Office extraction path. Structured fact extraction, LLM extraction filtering, and citation verification are proposed. Knowledge gap analysis (project-anchored, conceptual bridges) and the knowledge pulse governance surface are active.
